title: Malty Flaky Indian Cream Rasmalai
description: A malty and flaky Indian dessert featuring Thai chilli jam, this crowd-pleaser is a nut-free friendly version of the classic rasmalai.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Add the melted butter, milk, and malt extract to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the disks for 20 minutes or until they are golden brown.
While the disks are baking, prepare the cream by whisking together the milk, sugar, and Thai chilli jam in a saucepan.
Bring the cream mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Once the disks are ready, allow them to cool for 5 minutes before soaking them in the cream mixture.
